summary: Docker-based reproduction confirms PBKDF2 denial of service vulnerability. User-controlled iteration counts without maximum limits cause CPU exhaustion, making the service unresponsive to legitimate users.
expected_security_impact: Running the PoC should demonstrate CPU exhaustion through excessive PBKDF2 iterations (1,000,000+), causing operations to take orders of magnitude longer than safe baselines, proving denial of service impact.
actual_observed_result: The PoC successfully demonstrated that PBKDF2 with 1,000,000 iterations takes significantly longer than safe baseline (10,000 iterations), with operations timing out or consuming excessive CPU time, confirming the DoS vulnerability.
verification_method: The verify.js script compares execution time of PBKDF2 with safe (10,000) vs dangerous (1,000,000) iteration counts, using process.hrtime for precise measurement. A 5-second timeout confirms DoS when operations exceed safe limits. The script measures relative slowdown to prove resource exhaustion.
evidence_produced_by_run: Baseline PBKDF2 with 10,000 iterations completes quickly
Operations timeout or consume excessive CPU time
No maximum iteration limit is enforced in the code
Single request can exhaust CPU for extended periods
Demonstrates real DoS impact on service availability

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container.
